title.en: High‑Risk Medical Devices and In Vitro Diagnostics Ontology

description.en: Ontology modelling high-risk medical devices and in vitro diagnostic devices, including manufacturers, risks, incidents and corrective actions, based on MDR/IVDR regulations.

title.value: High‑Risk Medical Devices and In Vitro Diagnostics Ontology

comment.en: Device used to perform in vitro diagnostic tests (IVD).
Risk class categories defined by the IVDR regulation for in vitro diagnostic devices.
Medical device intended for single use.
Any high-risk medical device or in vitro diagnostic device registered in the EU catalogue.
Medical device that is both implantable and active.
Device for which a corrective action is ongoing.
Organization responsible for producing a medical device.
Medical device that relies on a power source for its operation.
Event reported in association with a device (e.g., adverse event, malfunction).
More specific risk classification (e.g., MalfunctionRisk, InjuryRisk).
Category of risk according to IMDRF taxonomy.
Device used by patients for self-monitoring.
Medical device intended to be totally or partially introduced into the human body.
Potential hazard identified for a device according to the IMDRF nomenclature.
Implantable medical device that relies on a power source for operation.
Field safety corrective action (FSCA) taken as a response to an incident.
Device belonging to the highest risk classes (IIb, III or D).
Risk class categories defined by the MDR regulation.
Device intended for use in diagnosis, prevention, monitoring, treatment or alleviation of disease.
Type of corrective action (SafetyAlert, Recall, etc.).
Indicates whether the device is active.
Relates a medical device to its MDR risk class.
Date when the incident was detected.
Relates an incident to the corrective action taken.
Indicates whether the device is implantable.
Relates an IVD device to its IVDR risk class.
Date when the corrective action was issued.
Indicates whether the device is reusable.
Country where the manufacturer is located.
Authorization number granted by the competent authority.
Date when the incident event occurred.
Indicates whether the device is an in vitro diagnostic device.
Regulatory framework (MDR or IVDR) under which the device is classified.
Links a corrective action to the incident it addresses.
Status of the corrective action (Open, Closed).
Date when the corrective action was closed.
Links a device to an incident reported about it.
Countries affected by the corrective action.
Single Registration Number assigned in EUDAMED.
Links a device to its manufacturer.
Identifier of the device (e.g., catalogue number).
Associates a device with a risk identified in the IMDRF catalogue.
Date when the incident was reported.
EUDAMED reference for the corrective action.
Links a risk to its type.
Links a risk to its category.
Country where the incident was reported.
